question.text: A protection strategy against longevity risk includes:

answer.text: Investing only in short-term deposits that mature every year
Purchasing a deferred annuity that begins paying income in mid-to-late retirement
Keeping all retirement savings in cash
Withdrawing the entire NPS corpus as lump sum

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A deferred annuity that begins paying at, say, age 80 or 85 provides guaranteed income in late retirement when other sources may have been exhausted. This specifically addresses longevity risk. **The Trap:** Short-term deposits (Option A) don't provide lifetime income guarantee. Lump sum withdrawal (Option D) removes the longevity protection built into NPS. **Cross-Exam Context:** Longevity risk management through deferred annuities is covered in Series XVII. NPS mandates minimum 40% annuitization partly for this reason. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Recommend purchasing a deferred annuity with a vesting date of 80-85 years for clients concerned about outliving their savings. This ensures income in the most vulnerable years.
